completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All tests pass with `pytest tests/ -m unit -v`
completion_promises.evidence: Added 'unit' marker to pyproject.toml and applied pytestmark=pytest.mark.unit to all five test files. Running `pytest tests/ -m unit -v` results in 196 passed, 395 deselected in 0.29s.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] No tests use `pytest.fixture` with mutable state — use factory functions from `conftest.py`
completion_promises.evidence: Grep for 'pytest.fixture' in tests/ returns no fixtures with mutable state in the five relevant test files. All test data is created via factory functions (_make(), _make_heartbeat(), _make_manifest(), etc.) and conftest.py factory helpers (make_nats_config, make_envelope, etc.).

Created tests/test_events.py with TestHeartbeatMetadataMutableDefaultIsolation class containing 6 tests: (1) metadata defaults to empty dict, (2) two instances have distinct metadata objects (is not), (3) mutating one does not affect other, (4) metadata field uses default_factory, (5) metadata accepts arbitrary values, (6) many instances all independent. Also tests active_workflow_states isolation.

TestManifestRegistryAbstract class in test_manifest.py contains test_cannot_instantiate_directly() which asserts `with pytest.raises(TypeError): ManifestRegistry()`. Also verifies abstract methods and InMemoryManifestRegistry subclass relationship.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `test_topics.py` covers wildcard rejection scenarios from BDD spec
completion_promises.evidence: TestNegative class in test_topics.py contains test_identifier_with_wildcard_star_rejected() and test_identifier_with_wildcard_gt_rejected() which verify that identifiers containing '*' or '>' raise ValueError with match='(?i)wildcard|invalid'. These map directly to BDD Scenario 'Identifiers containing NATS wildcards are rejected'.

test_config.py uses monkeypatch.setenv in 5 tests: test_env_url_override, test_env_user_password, test_env_creds_file, test_env_precedence, test_instances_independent. test_agent_config.py also uses monkeypatch.setenv in 6 tests for AGENT_* env vars. All env var tests correctly use monkeypatch.setenv (not os.environ).

Running pytest with --cov=nats_core.config --cov=nats_core.topics --cov=nats_core.events --cov=nats_core.manifest --cov=nats_core.agent_config --cov-fail-under=90 reports TOTAL 95.37% coverage. Per-module: config 92%, topics 99%, manifest 100%, agent_config 100%, events/__init__ 100%, events/_agent 100%, events/_fleet 100%, events/_jarvis 100%, events/_pipeline 83%. Overall 95% exceeds the 90% threshold.
